phase_0_decision: Phased rollout 1->2: PRD targets Option 1 (drift-reactive auto-isolation) for Phase 1 + Option 2 (concurrency-proactive, advisory->enforced calibration) for Phase 2. Option 3 (always-isolate) deferred as end-state. Operator-approved at Phase 0 gate 2026-06-06.

metrics.kill_criteria: KC1 drift not dropping in 14d -> revert advisory; KC2 false-trigger >25% or kill-switch >=2x -> narrow predicate; KC3 any uncommitted-work loss -> immediate revert (hard stop).
metrics.kill_criteria_resolution: Not triggered. Advisory-first both phases; KC3 structurally prevented + tested (stash never dropped until apply verified) across 19 tests. KC1/KC2 evaluated at T+7d/T+14d calibration; revert is a single flag flip.

brainstorm.problem_statement: Branch isolation today triggers only on file PATH (infra-path globs, via BRANCH_ISOLATION_VIOLATION Mode B/C) and is only REACTIVELY DETECTED after drift has already flipped HEAD (W9 alert). There is no trigger keyed on concurrency or on drift itself, and non-infra work (features/enhancements/tasks/chores) gets NO proactive isolation. In the operator's dominant workflow — multiple concurrent Claude sessions sharing one SSD checkout — a sibling session's `git checkout` flips the shared HEAD, so an unprotected session's commits land on the wrong branch.
problem_alternatives: User-stated: W9 only warns; make it auto-isolate so the operator stops recovering drift by hand.
Behavioral: concurrent sessions on one shared checkout flip shared HEAD via git checkout; non-infra work isn't isolated so commits land on the wrong branch.
Underlying need: each concurrent agent needs a private, stable repo view so its commits land where intended regardless of sibling HEAD changes.

Strategic: the isolation defense was built for single-session path-based risk; the operator's actual parallel-agent/shared-checkout workflow is a different threat model the defense silently doesn't cover.
brainstorm.empirical_evidence: Branch drift struck 3+ times on 2026-06-05/06 in one session: the #645 CI-fix commit landed on feature/3d-universe-phase-4c-act1-threshold; HEAD later flipped to main twice; 8 live worktrees + ≥1 concurrent session sharing the main checkout. Each recovery was manual (move commit to correct branch, restore sibling branch, restore HEAD).

three_option_matrix.dimensions_note: Infra feature (no UI). UX dimension = operator experience/ergonomics; Design dimension = integration surface with existing hooks/gates/config; Dev dimension = implementation footprint + risk.

options.name: drift-triggered (reactive)
options.outcome: When W9's PostToolUse:Bash hook detects HEAD changed AND the session has uncommitted/about-to-commit work, auto-dispatch isolation (move work into its own worktree via create-isolated-worktree.py) so the next commit can't land on the wrong branch.
options.ux: Operator sees a one-line 'drift detected -> isolating into worktree X' notice. Acts only after drift, so there is a (small) window between flip and detection. Lowest interruption — fires rarely, only on actual drift. May prompt a Touch ID tap if it commits/stashes.
options.design: Smallest integration surface: extends the EXISTING W9 hook (scripts/check-branch-drift.py) + reuses create-isolated-worktree.py + agent-leases.json. No change to the enforced BRANCH_ISOLATION_VIOLATION gate. No new settings.json hook.
options.dev: Add an isolation-dispatch branch to check-branch-drift.py; needs an atomic stash->worktree-add->pop sequence with a lock to avoid self-racing. ~1 script + tests. Reversible via env flag (CLAUDE_W9_DISABLE_DRIFT_CHECK already exists).
options.defer_to_v2: Proactive/concurrency-based isolation; non-infra extension of the enforced gate.
failure_modes: Reactive: a commit could still land wrong if it happens in the same tool-call as the flip (before the next PostToolUse fires).
Self-race: auto-creating a worktree mid-session could itself collide with a concurrent checkout.
Stash/pop of a dirty tree mid-edit could surprise the operator.

options.name: concurrency-triggered (proactive, lease-gated)
options.outcome: On first Edit/Write to feature code, check agent-leases.json for another LIVE lease on the same checkout; if concurrency is detected, auto-isolate BEFORE any drift can occur — extending BRANCH_ISOLATION_VIOLATION's auto-isolation flow to all work-types when (and only when) concurrency is present.
options.ux: Zero drift window — isolation happens before the first risky edit. Operator gets a worktree from the start of substantive work. Slightly more upfront friction (a worktree per concurrent session) but only when actually running in parallel; solo sessions are untouched.
options.design: Larger surface: extends the ENFORCED BRANCH_ISOLATION_VIOLATION gate to non-infra work-types gated on concurrency detection. Needs a reliable 'is another session live?' signal — lease-file freshness in agent-leases.json. Touches a load-bearing enforced gate -> mandatory advisory->enforced calibration window.
options.dev: Lease-freshness reader + gate extension + a PreToolUse/first-edit hook. Bigger test surface (concurrency simulation). Rollback = advisory-mode flag on the new trigger. Risk: false-negative if leases aren't refreshed, false-positive if stale leases linger.
options.defer_to_v2: Unconditional always-on isolation; auto-cleanup of spawned worktrees on merge.
failure_modes: Lease signal unreliable: stale lease -> false isolation; missing lease -> missed isolation (the failure we're trying to fix).
Extending an enforced gate risks blocking legitimate solo work if concurrency detection misfires.
Worktree proliferation when many short parallel sessions run.

options.name: always-isolate (unconditional)
options.outcome: Every session auto-isolates into its own worktree at session start, regardless of concurrency or work-type. Shared-checkout drift becomes structurally impossible.
options.ux: Maximal safety, zero drift ever. But maximal friction: every session — even a one-line solo chore — spins up + later cleans up a worktree. 8 worktrees already exist; this multiplies on-disk footprint and cleanup load on the SSD.
options.design: Largest surface: a SessionStart hook that always isolates, superseding both the path-based and concurrency-based triggers. Changes default dispatch behavior for ALL work unconditionally — highest blast radius.
options.dev: SessionStart auto-worktree + lifecycle/cleanup machinery (auto-remove on merge, GC orphans). Heaviest test + ops surface. Rollback = global disable flag. Risk: SSD disk pressure, worktree GC bugs, slower session starts (~200-500ms + disk per worktree).
options.defer_to_v2: N/A — this IS the end-state; nothing deferred.
failure_modes: Worktree proliferation + SSD disk pressure (SanDisk Extreme disconnect bug already a known hazard).
Cleanup debt: orphaned worktrees accumulate without robust GC.
Friction tax on every solo session may push the operator to disable it entirely -> worse than a targeted trigger.

three_option_matrix.recommendation: NONE — per three-option contract, the matrix is the output; operator picks. (Author's note for the gate: options compose as a phased rollout — 1 then 2 — rather than being mutually exclusive.)

tasks.title: Atomic isolation primitive: stash->worktree-add->pop with lock; no data loss
Tests: drift->offer, dirty-tree no-data-loss, false-trigger, lock contention, kill-switch
Lease-freshness (TTL) reader over agent-leases.json: is-another-session-live signal
Extend BRANCH_ISOLATION_VIOLATION flow to all work-types, concurrency-gated, ADVISORY
First-edit trigger to run the concurrency check
Tests: concurrency sim (live vs stale lease), advisory telemetry, no-solo-isolation
Calibration wiring: advisory->enforced 4-criteria doc + T+14d review hook
(Open-Q2, if cheap) cleanup-on-merge worktree hook; else defer to GC track
tasks.defer_reason: open-Q2: cleanup-on-merge bundled with Phase 2 worktree-lifecycle work

tasks.title: Docs + v7.8.5: extend W9 observed-patterns + pattern-skill-map.json; gen-skill-preflight

transitions.note: Phased 1->2 scope approved at Phase 0 gate.
PRD approved as-is with 3 default open-question answers.
12-task breakdown approved; operator pre-approved driving to implementation.
Integration spec written (no UI); building Phase 1 slice T1-T5+T12.
Both phases merged (#648,#649); case study + calibration written.
